opportunities.title: We Fact-Checked Jimothy the Raccoon's Official D&D Stat Block (And It's Wrong)
opportunities.why_now: A viral animal just got an official 5e-style stat block, and stat-block accuracy is exactly the kind of rules minutiae this channel exists to adjudicate.
opportunities.angle: Line-by-line rules breakdown: compare Jimothy's CR, AC, and abilities against actual Monster Manual math for a same-sized creature, then rule on whether the stats hold up

opportunities.title: Chaosium's Innsmouth Book Just Became Canon: Here's What Changed Mechanically
opportunities.why_now: A 'definitive guide' claim on a classic Lovecraft setting demands a rules-first verdict on what's actually new crunch vs. repackaged lore.
opportunities.angle: Rules audit post: pull the new stat blocks, sanity mechanics, and location rules and rank them against prior Cthulhu supplements

opportunities.title: Free Adventure Alert: 'Thief of Princes' — Is It Actually Balanced for Your Table?
opportunities.why_now: A free adventure just dropped, and free content gets clicks fast before the window closes.
opportunities.angle: Quick-hit rules review: encounter math, DC checks, and treasure parcels checked against DMG guidelines, with a verdict on level range

opportunities.title: The Rogue Rules Nobody Reads: What 'Cloaks and Daggers' Gets Right (and Wrong) About Stealth Mechanics
opportunities.why_now: A fresh EN World piece on cloak-and-dagger tables is a direct hook to litigate stealth, disguise, and hide-in-shadows rules that always spark table arguments.
opportunities.angle: Rules-first breakdown of hiding/stealth mechanics across editions, using the article as a jumping-off point for 'here's what the rules actually say'

opportunities.title: 'A Spoonful of Sugar' and the Math of Mayhem: How Chaos Mechanics Actually Work
opportunities.why_now: The RPG Evolution column just tackled mayhem/chaos systems, a perfect excuse to break down the underlying dice mechanics for GMs who want the rules, not the vibes.
opportunities.angle: Analytical post decoding the probability and design intent behind 'mayhem' subsystems referenced in the column
